behaviors.description: Cron parser rejects invalid expressions with descriptive errors.
behaviors.justification: MOCK-MISS: failure is local parsing/validation logic, not module handoff. REAL-INFRA: no OS/network/subprocess needed. LLM-DEP: none. Deterministic-plumbing proof: behavior says parser "rejects invalid expressions ... with a descriptive error message". Model-output exclusion proof: inputs are fixed syntax values ("month 13, day 32, malformed syntax"), with no AI/ML judgment. Rejection-gate: rejected Integration because behavior text targets one component ("cron parser"); rejected System/Agentic/Workflow because nothing in the behavior requires real infra, model calls, or multi-agent steps.
behaviors.test_description: Call parser directly with invalid cron strings and assert structured error codes/messages include offending field/value; table-drive malformed token, out-of-range month/day, and wrong field count cases.

behaviors.description: Next-run-time calculator returns correct timestamps for standard and edge cron semantics.
behaviors.justification: MOCK-MISS: core risk is date-calculation algorithm correctness. REAL-INFRA: can be reproduced fully in-process with fixed clock/start time. LLM-DEP: none. Deterministic-plumbing proof: behavior says calculator "returns correct timestamps" for cron rules. Model-output exclusion proof: edge cases are explicit deterministic rules ("last day of month", "nearest weekday"). Rejection-gate: rejected Integration since behavior is calculator correctness, not cross-module state handoff; rejected System/Agentic/Workflow because no real OS/network/model/pipeline dependency appears in the behavior text.
behaviors.test_description: Use deterministic start instants and prebuilt schedule objects (or parsed fixtures) to assert the exact next N timestamps for normal and edge expressions, including month-length/leap-year boundaries.

behaviors.description: Overlap detector flags schedule pairs violating minimum gap and reports conflict windows.
behaviors.justification: MOCK-MISS: real failure commonly appears at module boundary between occurrence generation and overlap-window reporting. REAL-INFRA brake: no real OS/network/subprocess required, so not System. LLM-DEP brake: no AI/ML output dependence. MULTI-STEP brake: no agent pipeline. Deterministic-plumbing proof: behavior is rule-based ("within the configured minimum gap ... reports the conflicting time windows"). Model-output exclusion proof: all decisions are numeric time comparisons, no model content quality. Rejection-gate: rejected System/Agentic/Workflow because behavior text is deterministic schedule math with local module wiring only.
behaviors.test_description: Wire parser/schedule-expander/overlap-detector together in-process; feed two cron schedules plus 5-minute threshold and assert detected conflict intervals and non-conflict boundaries around exactly-equal-gap and off-by-one-minute cases.

behaviors.description: Timezone-aware scheduling handles DST spring-forward gap and fall-back ambiguity for 2:30 AM jobs.
behaviors.justification: MOCK-MISS: failure emerges from interaction of cron recurrence logic with timezone/DST resolution rules. REAL-INFRA brake: can run in-process using real timezone library + tz database; no external OS event/network/subprocess needed. LLM-DEP brake: none. Deterministic-plumbing proof: behavior requires "correct run times across a DST transition" for a fixed local time. Model-output exclusion proof: DST handling is calendar-rule resolution ("spring-forward gap"/"fall-back ambiguity"), not AI judgment. Rejection-gate: rejected System because behavior does not require real OS clock changes, only timezone-rule computation; rejected Agentic/Workflow due no model calls.
behaviors.test_description: Integration test with real timezone data (e.g., America/New_York), fixed start dates around DST boundaries, and assertions for nonexistent local time handling in spring and duplicate local time disambiguation in fall.

behaviors.description: Engine applies missed-run policy (skip vs backfill) when computed next run is in the past.
behaviors.justification: MOCK-MISS: failure is policy decision at handoff between next-run computation and execution-planning state. REAL-INFRA brake: no real OS/network/subprocess required for this decision. LLM-DEP brake: none. MULTI-STEP brake: no agentic chain. Deterministic-plumbing proof: behavior states engine "determines whether to skip or backfill based on ... configured missed-run policy". Model-output exclusion proof: outcome is policy enum + timestamp comparison, no model output content. Rejection-gate: rejected Unit only if policy is fully isolated; behavior text explicitly couples "computed next run time" with policy action, implying module interaction. Rejected System/Agentic/Workflow as behavior contains no real infra or model dependence.
behaviors.test_description: Integrate schedule calculator with policy evaluator and job metadata; simulate downtime by advancing clock beyond expected run, then assert produced execution plan differs correctly for `skip` vs `backfill` policies (including backfill count/window limits if configured).
